title_original: Cambridge launches major strategic partnership with IonQ to
summary_original: The University of Cambridge has initiated its largest-ever corporate research partnership with United States-based IonQ, bringing the UK’s most powerful quantum computer—a 256-qubit IonQ system—to the new IonQ Quantum Innovation Centre at the Ray Dolby Centre. Supported by Innovate UK and the National Quantum Computing Centre, this partnership will provide researchers and early-stage companies UK-wide with access and computing time over three years. Led by figures such as Professor Mete Atatüre and Vice-Chancellor Professor Deborah Prentice, the collaboration promises long-term investment in quantum science, spanning quantum computing, sensing, networking, and security, and aims to strengthen Cambridge’s role in the UK’s national quantum programme.
title_fixed: Cambridge launches major strategic partnership with IonQ to
summary_fixed: The University of Cambridge has initiated its largest-ever corporate research partnership with United States-based IonQ, bringing the UK’s most powerful quantum computer—a 256-qubit IonQ system—to the new IonQ Quantum Innovation Centre at the Ray Dolby Centre. Supported by Innovate UK and the National Quantum Computing Centre, this partnership will provide researchers and early-stage companies UK-wide with access and computing time over three years. Led by figures such as Professor Mete Atatüre and Vice-Chancellor Professor Deborah Prentice, the collaboration promises long-term investment in quantum science, spanning quantum computing, sensing, networking, and security, and aims to strengthen Cambridge’s role in the UK’s national quantum programme.
